 {"id":"bd-vyt6","title":"Add current-engine terminology erratum to comprehensive spec and fix ext4-only top-line wording","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md still opens with ext4-only wording and repeatedly uses FrankenFsEngine nomenclature that can be misread as the active FUSE runtime path. Add a focused errata bullet in §0.1.1 clarifying current code-path mapping () and adjust the top-line intro sentence to ext4+btrfs wording. Keep changes minimal and avoid broad spec rewrites.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:44:56.210135235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:45:18.135506890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:45:18.135487854Z","close_reason":"Completed focused spec errata note for engine terminology and ext4+btrfs intro wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","spec"]}
 {"id":"bd-w0pr","title":"Wire repair refresh lifecycle into OpenFs write path","description":"Spec §12.1.3 requires marking groups dirty + queued refresh after writes; ffs-repair QueuedRepairRefresh exists but ffs-core/ffs-block write path doesn't wire RepairFlushLifecycle. Integrate ArcCache with QueuedRepairRefresh + ScrubDaemon pipeline or equivalent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-13T18:55:03.478585707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:39:42.328256059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:39:42.324578806Z","close_reason":"Wired RepairFlushLifecycle into OpenFs commit paths. commit_transaction, commit_transaction_ssi, and commit_transaction_journaled now notify the repair lifecycle with committed block numbers, allowing repair subsystem to queue symbol refresh per spec §12.1.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-w1n6b","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse ioctl argument harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-fuse` ioctl dispatch boundary. Build a fuzz harness around `dispatch_ioctl(...)` / `dispatch_ioctl_for_testing(...)` that mutates `cmd`, `in_data`, `out_size`, `fh`, `caller_pid`, and read-only/writeable mount options while using the existing recorder/test doubles. The goal is to stress the argument parsers and ioctl response encoding without needing a live mount.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1213-1353` FIEMAP parsing/encoding helpers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1241-1300` move_ext / u32 / flag parsers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1536-1833` ioctl dispatch for FIEMAP, GET/SETFLAGS, GET/SETVERSION, fscrypt, move_ext, fslabel, and btrfs ioctl cases

Mutation classes:
- short/oversized ioctl buffers
- inconsistent `out_size` vs payload sizes
- garbage command numbers / incompatible flag bits
- malformed fscrypt `_EX`, move_ext, FIEMAP, and fslabel argument layouts

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- harness proves no panic across malformed ioctl argument shapes
- any crashers become regression tests next to the current unit coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:19Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-fuse","fuzzing","ioctl","mount-path","testing"]}

Create target exercising directory block manipulation and hash computation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:06:15.085098289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:06:15.085098289Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Create target exercising directory block manipulation and hash computation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:06:15.085098289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:05:10.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:05:10.000000000Z","close_reason":"Implemented fuzz_dir_operations with valid dir-block setup, raw-block mutation, remove_entry determinism, dx_hash determinism, and htree insert/remove/leaf lookup invariants; verified with remote cargo check and clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-w3pv","title":"Implement btrfs FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE support","description":"Spec-first parity task: extend the experimental btrfs RW contract to support FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE semantics, add deterministic unit/E2E coverage, and update parity/docs in the same patch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:54:33.978700418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:07:34.878773336Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:07:34.878274881Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: ZERO_RANGE support in both ext4 and btrfs fallocate paths. 2 tests passing (zero_range_zeroes_data, zero_range_keep_size). FEATURE_PARITY already tracks this.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fallocate","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-w8amw","title":"Freeze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug contracts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T04:51:31.106341415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.203547933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.201452016Z","close_reason":"Froze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug output; verified with focused rch tests plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wch7b","title":"add mounted-path btrfs user.xattr XATTR_CREATE/XATTR_REPLACE errno coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` currently proves basic set/get/list/remove semantics, but not the operator-visible `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` flag contract that `ffs-fuse` and `OpenFs::btrfs_setxattr` already support. This leaves create-vs-replace errno parity unproven on the public mount surface.

Scope:
- Reuse or extend the mounted-path xattr helper to issue flagged `setxattr` calls on a real btrfs FUSE mount.
- Assert `XATTR_CREATE` rejects existing names with `EEXIST` while preserving the original value.
- Assert `XATTR_REPLACE` rejects missing names with the stable missing-xattr errno (`ENODATA`/`ENOATTR`) and does not create the attribute as a side effect.
- Cover at least one regular-file path and keep the assertions independent of ext4-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- btrfs mounted-path tests prove create/replace errno semantics and no-side-effect guarantees.
- Any transport-level skips are explicit and distinguishable from backend regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:08.677704654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.040647817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.038372449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
